channel.title: CTV News – Queen's Park
channel.description: Queen's Park coverage from CTV News Toronto.

item.title: Ontario signs critical minerals agreement with the UK
Ontario's repealed wage restraint law Bill 124 still rippling through public sector
item.description: Ontario’s ill-fated wage restraint law known as Bill 124 is still sending ripples through the public sector across the province, even years after the law was repealed, with the electricity system now attempting to untangle the fallout

item.title: Ontario doctors voice concern on emergency department wait times
item.description: The Ontario Medical Association (OMA) is warning that the province’s emergency departments are already facing crowding as the busy summer season begins.

item.title: Ontario families struggling as community and social service workers strike enters fourth week
item.description: For the last three weeks, Toronto resident Owen Rees has filled notebooks with pleas to return home to his roomful of records, his friends down the hall, and a dance party every Friday.

item.title: NDP calls for audit into $1 billion given to private administrators of public services
item.description: Ontario’s opposition NDP is calling for the province’s Auditor General to audit three private companies who together got $1 billion to administer public services, including employment services and disability support.

item.title: Ontario adds 2 major ticket resale sites to consumer watchlist
item.description: A day after they were added to a provincially run consumer safety watchlist, Ticket resale sites SeatGeek and StubHub are saying the government has failed to provide them with enough clarity around a new law banning the resale of tickets above face value.

item.title: Taxpayers will spend $200 million on new Ontario Place parking garage
item.description: Ontario taxpayers will spend $200 million on a new parking garage at Ontario Place, but the government says it will quickly generate tens of millions of dollars in annual revenue.

item.title: ‘Reckless and disrespectful’: Pennsylvania governor slams Trump’s Canada comments as he signs trade pact with Ontario
item.description: Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro slammed the barrage of 'disrespectful' comments Donald Trump lobbed at Canada Wednesday as he met with Doug Ford and signed a partnership agreement on trade.

item.title: ‘Everyone’s going to be kumbaya’: Ford reiterates U.S. booze will go back on store shelves once trade deal is signed
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said again Tuesday that he won’t put U.S. alcohol back on LCBO shelves in Ontario until a trade deal is hammered out with the U.S. to end the ongoing trade war.

item.title: ‘They treat us so well’: Doug Ford responds after reception with American billionaire is cancelled
item.description: A planned reception with Doug Ford and American billionaire Ross Perot Jr. in D.C. was cancelled Monday, according to the U.S. Chamber of Commerce.

item.title: Housing advocate Eric Lombardi joins Ontario Liberal leadership race
item.description: Housing advocate Eric Lombardi is officially joining the race to lead Ontario’s Liberal party and “bring back an affordable life.”

item.title: Federal government launches public consultations on Billy Bishop airport
item.description: The federal government says it is launching consultations on the future of Toronto’s Billy Bishop airport as the Ford government moves to expand the airport into a busier facility that could accommodate higher passenger volumes, as well as jets.

item.title: Unionized workers in Ontario rally for lost wages, transparency on $1B spent on privately administered government programs
item.description: Hundreds of striking or locked-out public sector workers marched through Toronto’s streets earlier this week, as part of a coordinated series of rallies designed to show solidarity to some 4,000 workers provincewide.

item.title: Report says Billy Bishop airport expansion could impact thousands of homes along waterfront
item.description: A new report from city staff is warning that any expansion of Billy Bishop airport could result in major adverse impacts on housing, congestion, the environment and the waterfront as a whole.

item.title: Carney has not formed opinion on Ford's island airport expansion vision in Toronto
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney says he has not formed an opinion on Ontario Premier Doug Ford’s plan to expand Toronto’s island airport.
